async_execution.mechanism: Copilot CLI hooks run synchronously; no async execution documented

context_injection.mechanism: Copilot CLI hooks cannot inject context into the agent session

decision_control.mechanism: pre_tool_use_deny: Copilot CLI PreToolUse hooks can deny (block) the tool invocation; no allow or modify documented

handler_types.mechanism: Copilot CLI hooks execute shell commands only; no HTTP, LLM prompt, or agent handler types documented

hook_scopes.mechanism: Copilot CLI hooks are configured at a single scope level; no multi-scope configuration documented

input_modification.mechanism: Copilot CLI hooks cannot modify tool input before execution

json_io_protocol.mechanism: Copilot CLI hooks communicate via exit codes; no JSON stdin/stdout protocol documented

matcher_patterns.mechanism: Copilot CLI hooks fire on all events of their configured type; no per-tool filtering documented

permission_control.mechanism: Copilot CLI hooks can deny tool invocations (decision_control) but do not govern tool availability

providerExtensions.name: Six Hook Event Types
providerExtensions.description: Copilot CLI exposes six named hook events: `sessionStart` (on new or resumed session), `sessionEnd` (on session complete or terminate), `userPromptSubmitted` (when user submits a prompt), `preToolUse` (before any tool executes — can deny execution), `postToolUse` (after tool completes — result modification not supported), and `errorOccurred` (on agent execution error). Each event receives a JSON payload over stdin with a `timestamp`, `cwd`, and event-specific fields. Only `preToolUse` produces meaningful output: a `permissionDecision` field (`"allow"`, `"deny"`, or `"ask"`) with an optional `permissionDecisionReason`. Output from all other hooks is ignored.

providerExtensions.name: JSON Hook Configuration File
providerExtensions.description: Hooks are defined in a `.github/hooks/*.json` file (or inline in a plugin's `hooks.json`). The top-level structure is `{"version": 1, "hooks": { EVENT_NAME: [HOOK_OBJECT, ...] }}`. Each hook object has a `type` field (currently only `"command"` is documented), `bash` (Bash script path or inline), `powershell` (PowerShell script path or inline), optional `cwd` (working directory), optional `timeoutSec` (default 30), and optional `comment`. Multiple hooks of the same event type execute in array order.

providerExtensions.name: Pre-Tool-Use Deny Gate
providerExtensions.description: The `preToolUse` hook is the only hook type that can block agent execution. Returning `{"permissionDecision":"deny","permissionDecisionReason":"..."}` from the hook script causes the tool call to be rejected. The `"allow"` decision is the default (returning nothing or `"allow"` both allow). The `"ask"` value is documented in the schema but only `"deny"` is stated as currently processed. The hook receives `toolName` (e.g., `"bash"`, `"edit"`, `"view"`, `"create"`) and `toolArgs` (a JSON string of the tool's arguments).
